checks.note: All four citations verified: (1) GitHub issue #61 confirmed open at aharada54914/sdd-forge, title/body match the described absolute-vs-relative-path contract incompatibility; (2) reports/task-review/sdd-forge-mcp/attempt-1/round-3/manual-precheck-note.md exists and its round-1 sibling confirms the 'false-positive Risk/Workflow file-level grep' detail; (3) all 11 files reports/quality-gate/2026-07-05T021549Z-sdd-forge-mcp-T-001.md..T-011.md exist and each was grep-confirmed to carry the issue-#61 precheck-incompatibility note; (4) retrospective FP-001 reads verbatim '4 workflow phases (spec-review, impl-review, task-review, quality-gate) across the whole feature', matching the WFI's citation exactly.

Hypothesis names a specific mechanism -- three gates plus the inherited quality-verification-gate launch contract each require mutually unsatisfiable precheck contracts (absolute vs relative paths, different required file sets, different hash-freshness rules) -- and explains the downstream effect (no sanctioned fallback documented, so every run re-derives an ad-hoc procedure). A mechanism, not a restatement of the symptom.

Category: plugin-improvement. Root Cause Hypothesis, Proposed Change (Change Description), and Expected Effect scanned against the Section 2 forbidden-term list -- zero forbidden terms found; only correct generic substitutions appear ('specification review gate', 'design review gate', 'task decomposition review gate', 'quality verification gate', 'review gate plugin'). Problem Evidence citing raw paths/issue numbers is permitted by the guide.

Single row: Target File 'AGENTS.md' (verified to exist at project root, not under plugins/). Change Description names the exact subsection to add ('Review gate precheck fallback'), gives four numbered concrete steps, and states the fallback's applicability boundary.

Names a specific metric ('workflow phases that need an undocumented, re-derived manual fallback') with a quantitative target (4 per feature this period -> 0), phrased in generic plugin-improvement terms. Explicitly scopes the null effect on review gate round counts.
Exactly one Target-Metric named, Baseline: 4 (matches retrospective FP-001 frequency), Target: 0, Horizon: 'next feature run (issue #64, Phase 1)'. All required elements present.

Plan references the specific retrospective evidence mechanism (inspecting each review round directory for manual-precheck-note.md and checking citation of the documented AGENTS.md procedure) and restates the FP-001-derived baseline count (4).
Only Target File is 'AGENTS.md', a project-root workflow file. No path under plugins/ appears in the Proposed Change table.

findings.description: The WFI references pre-existing open issue #61 instead of describing a to-be-created issue. Judged acceptable: issue #61's confirmed title and body describe the exact same mutually-incompatible precheck-contract mechanism cited in this WFI's Problem Evidence and Root Cause Hypothesis, so referencing it is sound anti-duplication practice rather than a scope or process violation. No corrective action needed; recorded for the orchestrator's awareness only.
